title: Gorc
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{MultipleIssues|image|updatecontent=[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']], [[The New Essential Chronology]]}} {{Character |type=Dark Jedi |image=[[File:Gorc.jpg]] |name=Gorc |homeworld=[[Pzob/Legends|Pzob]]{{SW|url=news/barely-tolerable-alien-henchmen-of-the-empire-part-2|series=Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} |birth= |death=[[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' ''[[Sulon Star]]'', [[Ruusan]]''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' |species=[[Mutant|Mutated]] [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]]{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050609a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 5: Two Peas in a Pod}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height=Over 3 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Blue]]''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' |skin= |cyber= |families=[[Skunnt]] |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=[[Thok (Gamorrean guard)/Legends|Thok]] |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[Seven Dark Jedi]] *[[Brothers of the Sith]] |masters=*[[Cronal]] *[[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] *[[Sariss]] |apprentices= }} {{Quote|It is a mystery why one would call Gorc and Pic twins. Even though one is a miniature version of the other, they look nothing alike. They also don't battle alike. Pic is the energy and Gorc, the counter. They are the clashing balance of opposites: one, the voice; the other, the body. This combination is deadly.|Qu Rahn as a Force ghost, to Kyle Katarn|audio=QuRahn-GorcPic.flac|}} '''Gorc''' was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] native of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Pzob/Legends|Pzob]]. After leaving his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] with his older brother [[Thok (Gamorrean guard)/Legends|Thok]], Gorc traveled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], serving first as a test subject for a [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] named [[Pineas Pontak]], and later as an enforcer for the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]] on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. However, when the brothers found themselves in trouble after a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] fight, Gorc was sold out to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] by Thok. The Gamorrean was subsequently [[Mutant|mutated]] into [[Sithspawn/Legends|Sithspawn]] by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] "monster-maker" [[Cronal]]. As a result of his mutation, Gorc shared genes, abilities, and characteristics and had a symbiotic link through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] with his fellow test subject, the [[Kowakian monkey-lizard/Legends|Kowakian monkey-lizard]] [[Picaroon C. Boodle]]. Because of that connection, Gorc and Pic were eventually dubbed "the [[Brothers of the Sith]]." The "twins" were pressed into service of the [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] and, as part of his [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Seven Dark Jedi|team]], helped him in the search for the long-lost [[Valley of the Jedi]], the [[Vergence/Legends|source]] of great power which Jerec hoped to use for his own needs. In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Jerec sent Gorc and Pic to [[Mission to Sulon|deactivate]] the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]] [[8t88/Legends|8t88]]—a mission which the brothers easily completed aboard Jerec's personal cargo ship, the ''[[Sulon Star]]''. However, 8t88 was tracked by an aspiring [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Kyle Katarn]], who was trying to stop Jerec. Gorc tried to attack Katarn with his huge [[lightclub]], but was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by a shot in the face from Katarn's [[Bryar pistol/Legends|Bryar pistol]]. ==Biography== ===Early life=== [[File:Gorc_n_Pic.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Gorc often carried his "brother" Pic on his shoulder.]] Gorc was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] native of the Gamorrean colony [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Pzob/Legends|Pzob]]. A member of the [[Skunnt]] clan, Gorc and his older brother [[Thok (Gamorrean guard)/Legends|Thok]] both shared a rather uncommon trait among their [[Species/Legends|species]]—they were [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]. The two were initially oblivious to their powers, but Thok, generally regarded as the smarter of the two, eventually realized their true potential when he accidentally belched out a ball of fire into the snout of his rival [[Scumbo/Legends|Scumbo]]. Believing that they would be able to put their powers to good use and make fortune, Thok acquired a [[universal transliterator]] and took Gorc offworld. Gorc and his sibling first found work as guinea pigs for a [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] named [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Pineas Pontak]] on the planet [[Zlarbv IV]]. As part of his development of the [[Pontak Hypergland]], Pontak performed painful experiments on the pair, infusing their bodies with alien secretions, which had an unexpected side-effect of making Gorc's and Thok's muscles swell when they were enraged. Fed up with the Doctor's methods, the brothers eventually left him and moved to the desert planet of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. There, Gorc and his brother met a fellow Gamorrean named [[Ortugg]], who recruited them to serve as bodyguards for a notorious local [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]], the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba Desilijic Tiure]]. The two spent some time in Jabba's employ until one night in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley's]] [[Red Moon Saloon]]. During an incident that erupted there—which involved Thok, Gorc, seven barrels of [[Zelosian wine]], and a [[Kloo horn/Legends|Kloo horn]]—three [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]] were bludgeoned to death and five [[Ugnaught/Legends|Ugnaughts]] ended up being fricasseed. Gorc and Thok were captured and ended up in the custody of a local [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[darksider]] named [[Sariss]], who was better known as the "Prophetess". Gorc's older brother betrayed him in an effort to save his own life and told Sariss about Gorc's Force-sensitivity. However, this only piqued the Prophetess' interest and she took Gorc away. Gorc ended up in Imperial scientist [[Tuzin Gast]]'s [[Project Chubar]], a research effort intending to provide super-intelligent, loyal, and [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-sensitive [[Non-huMan|non-Humans]] to serve the Empire as [[Spy/Legends|spies]].''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]'' However, Gorc and the [[Kowakian monkey-lizard/Legends|Kowakian monkey-lizard]] [[Picaroon C. Boodle]] were soon taken by [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Cronal]], who needed two Force-sensitive "animal" subjects for his latest experiment. Cronal experimented on the pair using powerful [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]] and eventually turned them into [[Sithspawn/Legends|Sithspawn]]. Both Pic and Gorc were [[Mutant|mutated]] beyond the point of recognition, and they each shared the other's genes, abilities, and characteristics, and had a symbiotic link through the Force. After being mutated and experimented on, Gorc and his "twin" were handed over to the [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]], who, alongside Sariss, trained the two Sithspawn in the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. The twins became members of Jerec's [[Seven Dark Jedi|cadre]] of Dark Jedi, which, besides Sariss and Jerec himself, also included the young [[Epicanthix]] [[Yun]], the former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]], and the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Boc Aseca]]. Jerec was amused with Gorc and Pic and found them "quite an interesting pair".[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]] Gorc then constructed for himself a [[lightclub]] variant of the [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] which resembled the traditional Gamorrean [[thogk]] club, as well as a set of [[M'uhk'gfa]] [[Armor/Legends|armor]] for Pic. ===Search for the Valley of the Jedi=== {{Quote|Oh, good stab, Pic.''"
"''What do I always tell you, Gorc? Teamwork, teamwork.|Gorc and Pic, after the latter killed Rolanda Gron|}} [[File:GorcManual.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Gorc]] The "[[Brothers of the Sith]]" assisted Jerec in his search for the long-lost [[Valley of the Jedi]], a [[Vergence/Legends|Force nexus]] containing numerous Jedi and [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] souls. Jerec, who hoped to harness the valley's power in order to rebuild the collapsing Galactic Empire, eventually discovered that the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]] might have some clues as to its location. In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Jerec finally tracked Rahn to the planet [[Dorlo]], where the Jedi Master was on a mission alongside several members of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Jerec sent Maw, Sariss, and Yun to [[Mission to Dorlo|capture]] Rahn, but the Jedi Master escaped on a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]]. However, Rahn's [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was soon captured in the [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] of Jerec's personal [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]], the [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|''Vengeance'']]. Boc then docked a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] with the corvette, used [[coma gas]] to render everyone aboard unconscious, and transferred Rahn and the Rebels to the ''Vengeance''. Before Rahn woke up, Pic stole his lightsaber, which—unbeknownst to the monkey-lizard—had once belonged to Jedi [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]. Rahn and his Rebel companions were then escorted to the ''Vengeance''{{'s}} [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]], where Jerec started questioning them about the valley. One of the Rebels, the [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinian]] technologist [[Rolanda Gron]] defied Jerec, and the Miraluka ordered Gorc and Pic to [[Death/Legends|kill]] him. Just as Gorc was ready to strike the Rebel with his club-like lightsaber, Pic lunged unexpectedly at Gron, stabbing him in the [[Throat/Legends|throat]] with his [[Sith sword|Sith dagger]]. The remaining two Rebels—the linguistics expert [[Nij Por Ral]] and the young [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Duno Dree]]—were executed by Yun and Boc. Rahn was left alone and was beaten to his knees by the Dark Jedi. Still, the Jedi Master refused to tell anything, so Jerec used the Force to [[Drain Knowledge|rip information]] from Rahn's mind. Rahn then unexpectedly [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force-pulled]] Yun's lightsaber and charged Jerec's Dark Jedi, quickly defeating Boc, Maw, and Sariss. When Gorc tried to attack the Jedi, Rahn easily knocked Gorc's lightsaber out of the Gamorrean's hands. At that moment, Jerec intervened and killed Rahn. From Rahn's mind, Jerec discovered that the man he had killed years ago, a farmer named [[Morgan Katarn]], had found the valley and hidden the map leading to it in his home on the planet [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]]. To this end, Jerec hired the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]] [[8t88/Legends|8t88]] to find and decipher the map. After the droid completed his objective, Jerec told him to go to Jerec's personal cargo ship, the ''[[Sulon Star]]'', to receive his reward. In reality, Jerec sent Gorc and Pic to [[Mission to Sulon|dispatch]] the droid; the twins were also ordered to kill [[Kyle Katarn]], the son of Morgan Katarn and an aspiring Jedi, who was trying to stop Jerec.{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/kylekatarnstale4|series=Kyle Katarn's Tale|text=Mission 4}} 8t88 soon arrived to the ''Sulon Star'' on a shuttle and found Pic and Gorc in the wardroom; pointing out that the Dark Jedi could sneak upon anyone, Gorc remarked that Jerec had taught them well. 8t88 asked for his pay, and Pic sardonically told him they had it before [[Sai cha/Legends|decapitating]] the droid. ===Showdown=== {{Quote|You defeated our friend Yun in saber combat. We are better than Yun.''"
"''But not smarter! ''[Shoots]'' Especially not when you walk right into a blaster shot.|Gorc and Kyle Katarn, aboard the ''Sulon Star''|}} [[File:GorcAndPic-JKDFII.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Gorc and Pic on the ''Sulon Star'']] Under the tutelage of the late Rahn's [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]], Kyle Katarn defeated Yun and tracked 8t88 to the ''Sulon Star'', looking for the information which would lead him to the Valley of the Jedi and Jerec. Katarn arrived at the corridor 8t88 had been beheaded in, where Gorc and Pic were hiding in the shadows. As Katarn moved toward the droid, its head fell off its shoulders, landing on the floor. Gorc then emerged from the shadows and taunted Katarn, saying they knew he would like to see the droid one last time, took it's head and fed to [[Grendel]] his pet [[Hornagaunt]], who digested it. attempted to crudely attack Katarn with his large weapon. However, Gorc overestimated the ease with which he would be able to defeat his foe and showed his lack of intelligence by standing in the way of Katarn's [[Bryar pistol/Legends|Bryar pistol]]. Katarn shot Gorc in the face, destroying the Gamorrean's brain and killing him instantly. Gorc's death drove Pic insane, and he attacked Katarn with his Sith dagger, but was killed shortly thereafter. ===Legacy=== Katarn ultimately managed to [[Mission to Ruusan|kill]] Jerec and free the spirits of the Jedi trapped in the valley. The young [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] regarded his duel with Pic and Gorc as extremely difficult and felt angry at the Rebel Alliance's leader [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] for not fully recognizing his effort.''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' Katarn left Gorc and Pic's bodies on the ''Sulon Star'', which eventually crashed near the valley. Years later, when Jedi Master [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] discovered that Pic had been in possession of one of Yoda's lightsabers, he sent a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of Jedi to retrieve it. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|A creature of few words. How refreshing.|Qu Rahn|}} [[File:Katarn_VS_Gorc.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Gorc facing to Katarn's Bryar pistol]] In many ways a typical representative of his species, Gorc was a mean individual with a love for fighting and a distaste for droids. A [[Color/Legends|blue]]-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]] Gamorrean, Gorc was very heavy and slow, being over three meters in height and weighing 200 kilograms. In contrast to his size, Gorc was somewhat dim-witted, even more-so than his older brother, a trait he displayed by standing in the way of Katarn's [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. Gorc preferred not to engage in conversation, which he usually left to Pic. He was the less aggressive of the pair, but that did not make him any less dangerous, as he was not afraid to slaughter his enemies. Over the years, Gorc and Pic's closeness became more than just a dark side connection—it could almost be described as friendship. Pic, who considered himself the "older" brother, deeply cared about his slower sibling and protected him from abuse. In return for his kindness, Gorc gave Pic the set of M'uhk'gfa armor which he had crafted himself. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|Gorc has been here, hidden within the shadows, hidden within the dark side.|Gorc|}} Gorc and Thok's Force-sensitivity manifested itself even back when they lived on Pzob. Their skin tended to acquire a unique reddish tint when they boiled with rage. Later, as a side-effect of Doctor Pontak's experiments, Gorc's muscles also started to swell dramatically whenever the Gamorrean became enraged. Trained by Jerec and Sariss in the ways of the dark side, Gorc and Pic—despite their nearly comical appearance—were a deadly pair. Pic was quick, agile, and intelligent and Gorc was extremely powerful. Gorc rarely moved or spoke, though when Pic stirred him up, the duo worked together as a team, covering all angles as well as each other's weaknesses.''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' manual Gorc had a long and thick orange-bladed lightclub and used it to deliver slow yet powerful strikes at his enemies, whom he preferred to immobilize with Force Grip first. When hit, Gorc was able to use the Force to [[Force healing/Legends|heal]] himself. Gorc could also hide his presence within the dark side, thus avoiding being detected by other Force-sensitives. He displayed this ability on the ''Sulon Star'' while hiding from Katarn. The bond between Pic and Gorc allowed them to be aware of each other's location, sense, and feelings at any given time. It also boosted their powers when the twins were near each other. However, such bond also had negative effects; every time one brother used the dark side, he further corrupted the other one, and vice versa. Also, every time one Sithspawn was injured, the other was forced to share his pain. Although he rarely communicated, Gorc was able to speak three languages—[[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], the Vregg dialect of the [[Gamorrese|Gamorrean language]], and the [[Sith (language)|Sith]]. Apart from his usage of the lightclub, Gorc was also proficient with a wide variety of other weapons, including [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]], [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifles]], [[Vibro-weapon/Legends|vibroweapons]], and even some primitive and simple weapons. Despite his slow wit, Gorc was also a masterful craftsman, single-handedly constructing Pic's suit of M'uhk'gfa armor and his own lightclub. ==Equipment== Gorc's weapon of choice was a [[lightclub]] variant of the lightsaber which resembled the traditional Gamorrean [[thogk]] club and constructed himself. For protection, he wore a chest armor, helmet and chin guard. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Gorc-JKconcept.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Gorc character concept art]] Gorc first appeared in the [[1997]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'', where he was realized entirely by computer-generated imagery. Gorc is the only Dark Jedi in the game that does not speak. In the game's credits, he is credited as "himself." Gorc later appeared in the [[1998]] novella adaptation of the first part of the game, ''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'', and its [[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|audio drama]] adaptation. Gorc's silent nature is preserved in the novel, where he only grunts. In the audio drama, however, Gorc has several lines and is voiced by [[Gary Groomes]].''[[Dark Forces: The Collector's Trilogy]]'' audio drama'' Additionally, Gorc was briefly mentioned in the second part of the game's adaptation, the ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' novel and [[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|audio drama]]. Gorc's backstory was expanded in ''[[The Dark Forces Saga]]'', a series of game scenarios released by [[Wizards of the Coast]] on their [[Wizards.com|web site]]. Gorc's death is different in ''Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II'' and in ''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent''. In the game, Katarn fights both Gorc and Pic with his lightsaber and any one of them can be killed first. In the novel, Katarn shoots Gorc in the face with his blaster, just as Gorc emerges from the shadows. According to [[Abel G. Peña]], Pic and Gorc may have been inspired by [[Wikipedia:Danny DeVito|Danny DeVito]] and [[Wikipedia:Arnold Schwarzenegger|Arnold Schwarzenegger]] in the [[1988]] comedy [[Wikipedia:Twins (1988 film)|''Twins'']], with one of the villains being tiny and the other huge.{{Blog|abelgpena|6|Sithspawn!}} When Abel Peña was writing the backstory for Gorc and his companion for the ''Dark Forces Saga'', he re-used the term Sithspawn, originally a curse word used by [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]] in ''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]'' which was revealed to mean a monstrosity created though dark side magic in ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''. Peña brought back the concept of Sithspawn, coupling two of the aliens seen in [[Jabba's Palace/Legends|Jabba's Palace]] during [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] to create the "Brothers of the Sith." The article [[Building a Better Jedi: Justin Chin on Dark Forces II|''Building a Better Jedi'']] that appeared in [[Star Wars Insider 31|''Star Wars Insider'' 31]] provided Gorc's height as 8 [[Foot/Legends|feet]], which roughly equals 2.4 meters.{{WebCite|url=http://www.google.ru/intl/eng/help/features.html|text=Google unit converter|nobackup=1}} However, his entry in ''The Dark Forces Saga'' later contradicted that information, stating that Gorc was over 3 meters tall. This article uses the more recent ''The Dark Forces Saga''{{'s}} height. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Gorc}} *''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' *[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]] *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' {{Mo}} *[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]] {{Mo}} ==Sources== {{ScrollBox|content= *{{InsiderCite|31|Building a Better Jedi: Justin Chin on Dark Forces II|Building a Better Jedi: Justin Chin on ''Dark Forces II''}} *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' *{{LucasArts|url=static/jk/html/characters.html|text=Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II - Characters}} *[[The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 2: Unsung Heroes of the Light}} {{Imo}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/kylekatarnstale4|series=Kyle Katarn's Tale|text=Mission 4}} Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 4: Of the Crudest Matter}} {{Imo}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/kylekatarnstale5|text=Light and Dark: Kyle Katarn's Tale, Mission 5|int=Kyle Katarn's Tale}}
